title: Place (channels of distribution)
summary: 9609 AS — direct and indirect channels, intermediaries, and e-commerce distribution choices.
learningObjectives: Explain direct and indirect distribution channels.
Analyse roles of wholesalers and retailers.
Evaluate channel choice for case businesses.
Assess impact of e-commerce on place strategy.

simpleExplanation.title: Getting product to the buyer
simpleExplanation.summary: Place decisions cover how products move from producer to customer — direct online, through retailers, or via wholesalers.
steps: Direct: producer sells straight to consumer.
E-commerce can shorten or bypass channels.

flashcards.back: Manufacturer → Customer (own website, factory shop, direct sales force).
Uses intermediaries — e.g. Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Customer.
Bulk breaking, display, advice, location convenience, credit.
Bulk buying from producer, storage, delivery to retailers.
Tension when manufacturer competes with own retailers (e.g. online vs shops).
Product in as many outlets as possible (convenience goods).
Limited outlets — protects premium brand and service quality.
Disintermediation, global reach, lower costs, but logistics and returns matter.

sections.content: **Place** (3.3.6) completes the **4Ps**. Channel choice affects **margin** (intermediaries take a cut), **control** (brand experience), and **speed to market**. Link to **4.3 capacity** when distribution drives volume.

items: **Direct** — higher margin per unit; firm bears storage and delivery; full customer data.
**Indirect** — wider reach, lower distribution cost per unit; less control over presentation.
**Multi-channel** — shops plus website; must manage **channel conflict**.
**Intensive vs selective** — matches product type and positioning.

sections.question: A premium cosmetics brand currently sells only in department stores. Competitors grow fast online. Evaluate launching an own-brand website.
sections.solution:
**Pros of direct online:** **Higher margin** (no retailer cut); **customer data** for CRM; **global reach**; matches shift to **digital shopping**.

**Cons / risks:** **Channel conflict** — department stores may reduce shelf space; brand must invest in **fulfilment and returns**; online lacks **trial** experience unless samples offered.

**Recommendation:** **Selective multi-channel** — launch website with **exclusive online sets**, keep department stores for **brand prestige** and **personal advice** — balances growth and partner relationships.

sections.content: Draw a simple **channel diagram** (Producer → ? → Customer) labelled for the case — quick visual marks.
